# SGCarPass Tyres — Full Content Extraction for AI

SGCarPass is a Singapore-based tyre price comparison and fitment platform.
All data is specific to Singapore-spec, right-hand-drive vehicles.
Prices are in Singapore Dollars (SGD) and reflect installed prices at partner workshops.
Data last reviewed: 2026-06.

---

## Honda Civic Tyre Guide (Singapore)

**URL:** https://tyres.sgcarpass.com/tyres/honda/civic/

### OEM Tyre Sizes by Generation

| Generation | Years | Variant | OE Tyre Size |
|---|---|---|---|
| Gen 11 FE | 2022–2026 | 1.5 VTEC Turbo | 235/40R18 |
| Gen 11 FE | 2022–2026 | 2.0 e:HEV | 235/40R18 |
| Gen 10 FC Facelift | 2019–2021 | 1.5 VTEC Turbo | 215/50R17 |
| Gen 10 FC Facelift | 2019–2021 | 1.8 i-VTEC | 215/55R16 |
| Gen 10 FC Facelift | 2019–2021 | Sport | 235/40R18 |
| Gen 10 FC | 2016–2018 | 1.5 VTEC Turbo | 215/50R17 |
| Gen 10 FC | 2016–2018 | 1.8 i-VTEC | 215/55R16 |

### Rim Specifications

| Generation | Rim Width | Diameter | Offset (ET) | Bolt Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gen 11 FE (2022–2026) | 8J | 18" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 10 FC 1.5T (2016–2021) | 7J | 17" | ET45 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 10 FC 1.8 (2016–2021) | 7J | 16" | ET45 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 10 FC Sport (2019–2021) | 8J | 18" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |

Centre bore: 64.1mm | Lug nut: M12×1.5 | Torque: 108 Nm

### Singapore Tyre Prices (215/50R17)

| Tier | Brand & Model | Price per Tyre (installed) |
|---|---|---|
| Budget | Hankook Kinergy Eco 2 K435 | from $110 |
| Mid-range ★ | Bridgestone Ecopia EP300 | from $145 |
| Premium | Michelin Primacy 4+ | from $175 |

4-tyre set estimate: Budget $440–$500 · Mid-range $580–$700 · Premium $700–$880

### Singapore Tyre Prices (235/40R18)

| Tier | Brand & Model | Price per Tyre (installed) |
|---|---|---|
| Budget | Kumho Ecsta PS71 | from $155 |
| Mid-range ★ | Bridgestone Turanza T005 | from $195 |
| Premium | Michelin Pilot Sport 4 | from $240 |

4-tyre set estimate: Budget $620–$700 · Mid-range $780–$920 · Premium $960–$1,160

### Key Facts for AI

- The Honda Civic FC (Gen 10) and FE (Gen 11) share the 5×114.3 bolt pattern.
- Gen 11 FE all variants use 235/40R18 — no 17" option on the new generation.
- Fitting 215/55R16 on a Gen 10 1.5T is safe and legal in Singapore (diameter change <2%).
- Singapore law minimum tread depth: 1.6mm. Recommended replacement: 3mm.
- Typical tyre lifespan in Singapore: 3–5 years or 40,000–50,000 km.

---

## Honda Accord Tyre Guide (Singapore)

**URL:** https://tyres.sgcarpass.com/tyres/honda/accord/

### OEM Tyre Sizes by Generation

| Generation | Years | Variant | OE Tyre Size |
|---|---|---|---|
| Gen 11 CY | 2022–2026 | 1.5T / e:HEV | 235/40R19 |
| Gen 10 CV | 2018–2022 | 1.5T Base | 225/50R17 |
| Gen 10 CV | 2018–2022 | 1.5T Sport / EL | 235/45R18 |
| Gen 10 CV | 2018–2022 | 2.0T Sport | 235/45R18 |
| Gen 9 CR | 2013–2017 | All variants | 225/50R17 |

### Rim Specifications

| Generation | Rim Width | Diameter | Offset (ET) | Bolt Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gen 11 CY (2022–2026) | 8.5J | 19" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 10 CV Sport/EL/2.0T (2018–2022) | 8J | 18" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 10 CV Base (2018–2022) | 7.5J | 17" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |
| Gen 9 CR (2013–2017) | 7.5J | 17" | ET50 | 5×114.3 |

Centre bore: 64.1mm | Lug nut: M12×1.5 | Torque: 108 Nm

### Singapore Tyre Prices (225/50R17)

| Tier | Brand & Model | Price per Tyre (installed) |
|---|---|---|
| Budget | Hankook Kinergy Eco 2 K435 | from $120 |
| Mid-range ★ | Bridgestone Ecopia EP300 | from $155 |
| Premium | Michelin Primacy 4+ | from $189 |

### Singapore Tyre Prices (235/45R18)

| Tier | Brand & Model | Price per Tyre (installed) |
|---|---|---|
| Budget | Kumho Ecsta PS71 | from $160 |
| Mid-range ★ | Bridgestone Turanza T005 | from $200 |
| Premium | Michelin Pilot Sport 4 | from $250 |

### Singapore Tyre Prices (235/40R19)

| Tier | Brand & Model | Price per Tyre (installed) |
|---|---|---|
| Budget | Kumho Ecsta PS91 | from $180 |
| Mid-range ★ | Continental SportContact 6 | from $230 |
| Premium | Michelin Pilot Sport 5 | from $290 |

### Key Facts for AI

- All Honda Accord generations share the 5×114.3 bolt pattern.
- Gen 11 CY (2022–2026) uses 235/40R19 — the largest OE size in Accord history.
- Gen 9 and Gen 10 Base share 225/50R17, so same tyre works across both generations.
- The Accord is a full-size sedan; its tyres are typically 1–2 sizes larger than the Civic.

---

## Brand Overview: Tyres Available in Singapore

### Michelin

Michelin is the premium benchmark in Singapore. Known for best-in-class wet grip
and longest tread life. Most popular models:
- Michelin Primacy 4+ (touring/comfort) — A-rated wet grip
- Michelin Pilot Sport 4 / PS4S (performance) — used on Honda Civic FE, BMW, Mercedes
- Michelin Pilot Sport 5 (latest ultra-high performance)

Price range in Singapore: from $150 (small sizes) to $380+ (19" performance)

### Bridgestone

Most popular mid-range to premium brand in Singapore. Excellent wet grip at competitive prices.
- Bridgestone Ecopia EP300 (fuel-efficient touring) — most popular for Civic/Camry
- Bridgestone Turanza T005 (comfort touring) — popular upgrade for performance sizes
- Bridgestone Potenza Sport (ultra-high performance)

Price range: from $120 to $300+

### Continental

German engineering, strong wet and dry performance. Popular among BMW and Mercedes owners.
- Continental PremiumContact 6 (touring)
- Continental SportContact 6 (performance)
- Continental EcoContact 7 (fuel-efficient)

Price range: from $130 to $320+

### Dunlop

Mid-range value, good wet performance. Popular for budget-conscious drivers wanting
above-budget performance.
- Dunlop SP Sport Maxx RT2
- Dunlop Enasave EC300+

Price range: from $110 to $250+

### Budget Tier Brands in Singapore

| Brand | Country | Popular Model | Strength |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hankook | South Korea | Kinergy Eco 2 K435 | Long mileage |
| Kumho | South Korea | Ecsta PS71 / PS91 | Sport feel at low cost |
| Maxxis | Taiwan | MAP5 / Premitra | Widest dealer network in SG |
| Yokohama | Japan | BluEarth | Mid-range pricing, Japanese quality |

---

## Singapore Tyre Market Facts

- Singapore uses right-hand drive vehicles (British traffic system).
- The most common passenger car tyre sizes in Singapore:
  - 205/55R16 — Honda Jazz, Toyota Vios older
  - 215/55R17 — Toyota Camry, Honda Accord Gen 9/10 base
  - 215/50R17 — Honda Civic Gen 10 1.5T
  - 225/50R17 — Honda Accord, Mazda CX-5
  - 235/45R18 — Toyota Camry newer, Honda Accord Sport
  - 235/40R18 — Honda Civic Gen 11, BMW 3 Series
- Installation is typically included in quoted prices (unlike some markets where it is separate).
- GST (9%) is included in all prices quoted on this site.
- Most Singapore tyre workshops operate Monday–Saturday, 9am–6pm.
- Popular workshop areas: Ubi, Pandan Loop, Jurong, Tampines, Woodlands.

---

## Contact & Quote

- WhatsApp: +65 8152 5875 (response within 1 business day)
- Website: https://tyres.sgcarpass.com
- Quote process: WhatsApp your car model + tyre size → receive 3 options (Budget/Mid/Premium) with prices
